mapping.newAcsConcept: ItemDefinition, TileDefinition, TriggerDefinition, or CustomLibraryObjectDefinition depending on behavior
EntityProfile.stats plus skills/traits; extend domain if classic attributes need exact support
SpellDefinition plus TriggerDefinition actions; runtime currently has generic actions rather than all classic stat effects
DialogueDefinition nodes, trigger showDialogue actions, presentation intro text

implementationNotes: The current runtime supports dialogue, flags, giveItem, playMedia, playSound, teleport, changeTile, and setQuestStage trigger actions.

Classic ACS effects such as changing life force, power, or arbitrary traits should become future trigger actions or be represented temporarily by flags/quest stages/dialogue until stat mutation is implemented.

Original ACS things may map to different new object kinds: a Door can be a TileDefinition plus ExitDefinition; a Magic Bag is an ItemDefinition; a Do-All-Walk Space is a TileDefinition plus TriggerDefinition; a Store is likely a TileDefinition plus future shop interaction object.
